title: elymus nodosus subsp. caespitosus
intro: Elymus nodosus subsp. caespitosus, also known as a type of wild ryegrass, is a perennial grass species native to various regions around the world. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Elymus nodosus subsp. caespitosus include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help alleviate coughs and bronchitis. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, for its medicinal uses.
